target.title: fix(extensions): bound JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where a malformed or unexpectedly large API response from external services (Feishu, Discord, Google Meet, MS Teams, Tlon) could cause the gateway process to buffer an arbitrarily large payload into memory, leading to an out-of-memory crash.

## Why This Change Was Made

Recent work bounded response reads across most extensions to prevent OOM from oversized API responses. Several extensions still used raw `response.json()` without size limits. This change replaces those calls with the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` helper, which caps reads at a safe limit. Existing error handling patterns (try/catch, .catch() fallbacks) are preserved.

## User Impact

Gateway operators running Feishu, Discord, Google Meet, MS Teams, or Tlon channel plugins are protected from OOM crashes caused by unexpectedly large external API responses.

## Evidence

All 1136 tests pass across 84 test files covering the affected extensions. Build succeeds with no errors.
target.author: narahariraghava

target.title: fix(msteams): escape markup in mention display names
target.body: **Summary:** Three related correctness fixes in the Teams extension. (1) Mention display names containing `&`/`<`/`>` were embedded verbatim inside `` markup — a crafted name could break out of the mention tag. (2) The inbound Teams message HTML decoder applied `&` → `&` first, double-decoding `<` into `<` instead of the literal `<` the user typed. (3) The welcome-card greeting rendered unstyled because Adaptive Card TextWeight/TextSize enums are case-sensitive (`"Bolder"`/`"Medium"`) and the code was passing lowercase values that silently fall back to Default. ## What Problem This Solves **Fix 1 — mention display-name injection (`mentions.ts`)** `parseMentions` rewrites `@[Name](id)` into Teams `Name` mention tags. The display name was inserted verbatim. When a Teams user/bot display name contains markup metacharacters (`&`, `<`, `>`) — which are legal in Teams — the raw characters leak into the adaptive-card markup: - `"Tom & Jerry"` → `Tom & Jerry` — bare `&` is an invalid entity; the `` is interpreted as live markup. - `"AB"` → `AB` — breaks out of the mention tag entirely, corrupting the entity boundary. **Fix 2 — double-decode in HTML entity decoder (`graph-thread.ts`)** `stripHtmlFromTeamsMessage` decoded `&` → `&` *before* `<`/`>`. When Graph API escapes a literally-typed `<` from the user as `<`, the wrong order turns it into `<` (two decode passes), producing the wrong plain text. A message like `"The token is <APIKEY>"` became `"The token is "` instead of the correct `"The token is <APIKEY>"`. **Fix 3 — Adaptive Card enum case (`welcome-card.ts`)** The Teams Adaptive Card renderer treats `TextWeight` and `TextSize` as case-sensitive string enums (`"Bolder"`, `"Medium"`, …). Values not matching the schema's PascalCase fall back to `Default` silently. The welcome-card greeting passed lowercase `"bolder"` and `"medium"`, so it rendered without the intended bold/medium-size styling. ## Fix **Fix 1:** Add `escapeTeamsMentionText` that replaces `&` → `&`, `<` → `<`, `>` → `>` (ampersand first to prevent double-escaping) and apply it to the display name before building the `` tag. The `mentioned.name` field keeps the original un-escaped human-readable name. **Fix 2:** Move the `&` → `&` replacement to the *end* of the entity decode chain (after `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`, ` `) so it can never cause a second decode pass on other entities. **Fix 3:** Use the schema's PascalCase values `"Bolder"` and `"Medium"` instead of the lowercase forms. ## Evidence Real runtime proof — three fixes in one `node --import tsx` run on the PR branch: ```text ============================================================ FIX 1 — parseMentions: escape markup in mention display names ============================================================ INPUT: "Hello @[Tom & Jerry](28:abc-def-ghi)!" --- BEFORE (verbatim, broken markup) --- rendered tag : Tom & Jerry contains raw '&' : true contains raw '' : true markup-safe : false --- AFTER (real parseMentions output) --- formatted text : Hello Tom & <b>Jerry</b>! entity text : Tom & <b>Jerry</b> mentioned.name : "Tom & Jerry" --- AFTER assertions --- escaped '&' -> & : true escaped '<' -> < : true escaped '>' -> > : true no raw '' in tag : true markup-safe : true Escape-injection check ("AB" attempt): output text : Hi A</at><at>B! break-out prevented : true ============================================================ FIX 2 — stripHtmlFromTeamsMessage: & decoded last (no double-decode) ============================================================ INPUT HTML : The token is <APIKEY> --- BEFORE (wrong decode order: & first) --- result : "The token is " double-decoded (unwanted '<') : true --- AFTER (real stripHtmlFromTeamsMessage, & last) --- result : "The token is <APIKEY>" preserves literal < : true no unwanted '<' decoded : true no double-decode : true Plain & still decoded: "Message with & ampersand" → correct: true ============================================================ FIX 3 — buildWelcomeCard: Adaptive Card TextWeight/TextSize PascalCase enums ============================================================ First TextBlock in Adaptive Card body: type : TextBlock weight : "Bolder" size : "Medium" --- Assertions --- weight is "Bolder" (PascalCase, Teams-renderable) : true size is "Medium" (PascalCase, Teams-renderable) : true weight is NOT lowercase "bolder" (falls back to Default in Teams) : true size is NOT lowercase "medium" (falls back to Default in Teams) : true All three fixes verified in one real runtime run. ``` ## Tests **Fix 1 tests** — `extensions/msteams/src/mentions.test.ts` (red before fix, green after): - `escapes markup characters in mention tags and keeps entity text aligned` — `parseMentions("Hi @[Tom & Jerry](28:abc-123)")` produces `Tom & <b>Jerry</b>`; `entity.text` matches the formatted tag; `mentioned.name` keeps the un-escaped original. - `escapes closing at tags inside mention names` — name `"AB"` is escaped so `result.text` no longer contains `AB`. - Existing tests (plain names, multiple mentions, non-ASCII) continue to pass. **Fix 2 tests** — `extensions/msteams/src/graph-thread.test.ts` (red before fix, green after): - `does not double-decode escaped entities (decodes & last)` — `stripHtmlFromTeamsMessage("The token is <APIKEY>")` returns `"The token is <APIKEY>"` (not `""`). **Fix 3 tests** — `extensions/msteams/src/welcome-card.test.ts` (red before fix, green after): - `styles the heading with valid PascalCase Adaptive Card enum values` — the first TextBlock has `weight === "Bolder"` and `size === "Medium"`.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Preserve thread sessions across daily reset by default
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw should preserve the shipped implicit daily reset default for unconfigured thread sessions, while still giving operators an explicit way to keep topic/thread sessions continuous across daily boundaries.

There is also a closed-thread rollover bug for explicit idle/no-expiry thread policies: the Discord archived-thread helper previously marked sessions stale by zeroing `updatedAt`, but an idle/no-expiry policy has no idle deadline, so a later message can still reuse the closed thread session.

## Fix

- Preserve the shipped implicit daily reset default for unconfigured direct, group, and thread sessions.
- Document explicit topic/thread continuity with `session.resetByType.thread: { mode: "idle", idleMinutes: 0 }`.
- Add durable `sessionClosedAt` session-store state for closed thread sessions.
- Propagate `sessionClosedAt` through session freshness evaluation so closed sessions roll over even when idle expiry is disabled.
- Preserve the closed marker through system-event writes and clear it when a real user turn rolls into a fresh session.

## Evidence

Validation:

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 2 test files passed
  - 168 tests passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed

Current-head follow-up validation after sibling-path fix:

- **Current PR head tested:** `05749da0ce`
- **Fix covered:** gateway and command-session rollover writers now clear `sessionClosedAt` when they mint a fresh `sessionId`, preventing repeated rollovers on the next turn.
- **Focused acceptance command:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 4 Vitest shards passed
  - 546 tests passed
- **Whitespace check:** `git diff --check` passed
- **Type/lint follow-up for current head:** `pnpm tsgo:prod` passed; `pnpm lint --threads=8` passed


Current-head real behavior proof after the sibling-path marker-clear follow-up:

- **Current PR head tested:** `05749da0ce`
- **Runtime path tested:** real `initSessionState()` from this PR branch via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`.
- **Environment/data:** local clean checkout with temporary `sessions.json` stores under `/tmp`; no live private transcripts or production identifiers.
- **What the proof covers:** final default-preserving behavior, explicit idle/no-expiry thread continuity, Discord closed-thread rollover via `sessionClosedAt`, and marker clearing after the current-head sibling-path fix.

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json
{
  "head": "05749da0ce",
  "tempRoot": "openclaw-pr90226-proof-nCTMtC",
  "observations": {
    "unconfiguredThreadDailyDefault": {
      "previousSessionId": "daily-before-boundary",
      "sessionId": "2dc479df-d0b9-4260-9981-c6e633a40f7a",
      "isNewSession": true,
      "reusedPrevious": false,
      "resetTriggered": false,
      "previousEntryHadClosedAt": false,
      "newEntryClosedAt": null
    },
    "explicitThreadIdleNoExpiry": {
      "previousSessionId": "explicit-idle-before-boundary",
      "sessionId": "explicit-idle-before-boundary",
      "isNewSession": false,
      "reusedPrevious": true,
      "resetTriggered": false,
      "previousEntryHadClosedAt": false,
      "newEntryClosedAt": null
    },
    "closedDiscordThreadRollover": {
      "previousSessionId": "closed-discord-before-rollover",
      "sessionId": "c1cb048a-b723-404a-a37d-051e87749b29",
      "isNewSession": true,
      "reusedPrevious": false,
      "resetTriggered": false,
      "previousEntryHadClosedAt": true,
      "newEntryClosedAt": null,
      "archivedTranscriptCreated": true,
      "activeTranscriptStillPresent": false
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result:

- Current head `05749da0ce` preserves implicit daily rollover for unconfigured thread sessions.
- Current head `05749da0ce` preserves explicit `resetByType.thread` idle/no-expiry continuity across the daily boundary.
- Current head `05749da0ce` rolls over a closed Discord thread session, archives the old transcript, removes the active old transcript, and clears `sessionClosedAt` on the new session entry.

Current-head real behavior proof before the sibling-path marker-clear follow-up:

- **Current PR head tested:** `f53724f2ca59`
- **Runtime path tested:** real `initSessionState()` from this PR branch via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`.
- **Environment/data:** local clean checkout with temporary `sessions.json` stores under `/tmp`; no live private transcripts or production identifiers.
- **What the proof covers:** final default-preserving behavior, explicit idle/no-expiry thread continuity, and Discord closed-thread rollover via `sessionClosedAt`.

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json
{
  "head": "f53724f2ca59",
  "tempRoot": "openclaw-pr90226-proof-d7gIej",
  "observations": {
    "unconfiguredThreadDailyDefault": {
      "previousSessionId": "daily-before-boundary",
      "isNewSession": true,
      "reusedPrevious": false,
      "resetTriggered": false
    },
    "explicitThreadIdleNoExpiry": {
      "previousSessionId": "explicit-idle-before-boundary",
      "isNewSession": false,
      "reusedPrevious": true,
      "resetTriggered": false
    },
    "closedDiscordThreadRollover": {
      "previousSessionId": "closed-discord-before-rollover",
      "isNewSession": true,
      "reusedPrevious": false,
      "previousEntryHadClosedAt": true,
      "newEntryClosedAt": null,
      "archivedTranscriptCreated": true,
      "activeTranscriptStillPresent": false
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result:

- Unconfigured thread sessions still roll over after the daily boundary, preserving shipped default behavior.
- Explicit `resetByType.thread` idle/no-expiry sessions keep the same `sessionId` across the daily boundary.
- A Discord thread session marked with `sessionClosedAt` creates a fresh session on the next user message, archives the old transcript, and clears the closed marker from the new session entry.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord or Telegram bot conversation in production. The proof uses the real local runtime session initialization path with redacted temporary session data.

target.title: fix(zai): bound Z.AI endpoint-probe error body reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users configuring the Z.AI provider could exhaust host memory during endpoint auto-detection. When onboarding a Z.AI key, OpenClaw probes which Z.AI base URL and model the key can reach by POSTing to `/chat/completions` and reading the error body of any non-2xx response. A misbehaving, compromised, or hostile endpoint (including a self-hosted `baseUrl` override) can stream an arbitrarily large or never-ending error body, and the probe would buffer the **entire** body into memory before parsing — risking an out-of-memory crash of the OpenClaw host during a routine setup/detection step.

## Why This Change Was Made

The error path in `probeZaiChatCompletions` (`extensions/zai/detect.ts`) used an unbounded `await res.json()` to extract `error.code` / `error.message`. This change reads the body through the shared `readResponseWithLimit` helper (`openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`) with a 16 MiB fail-closed cap, then `JSON.parse`s the decoded prefix. On overflow the helper cancels the stream and throws; the pre-existing `try/catch` ("ignore malformed error bodies") swallows it and the probe degrades to its existing status-only fallback. This reuses the same bounded-read pattern already merged across providers (image-generation #96495, openrouter video #96873, exa, qqbot media) rather than introducing a new mechanism. Non-goal: detection results and error classification for well-formed responses are unchanged.

## User Impact

Users running Z.AI provider detection/onboarding are protected from OOM caused by oversized error responses: the probe reads at most 16 MiB of an error body and fails closed (graceful status-only fallback) instead of buffering unbounded. For every normal/well-formed response, behavior is identical — same detected endpoint, model, and error-code classification. Blast radius is low: one provider file plus its self-contained test; no shared infra, mocks, config, schema, or public API touched.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: an untrusted Z.AI probe error response with no `Content-Length` and an oversized/never-ending body must not be buffered whole; the read must stop near the cap, cancel the stream, and let the probe fall back — while valid small bodies still decode and classify unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: a real `node:http` server (`createServer`) bound to `127.0.0.1`, streaming a 400 JSON body in 64 KiB chunks with **no Content-Length** up to a ~24 MiB ceiling, driving the **real exported** `detectZaiEndpoint` over a real socket (a URL-rewriting `fetchFn` points the probe at the local server) and the **real** `readResponseWithLimit` the probe wraps. Node v22.22.0.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - Probe returns `null` (status-only fallback — the oversized body cannot promote the endpoint); the server observed the client **close the socket early** (stream cancelled); only **18,612,246 bytes** reached the wire vs the **25,165,824-byte** body — cancelled early, not drained.
  - `readResponseWithLimit(res, 16 MiB)` threw `Z.AI probe error body exceeded size limit (16777216 bytes)` and stopped at **18,481,174 bytes** (well below the full body).
  - **Negative control**: the OLD unbounded `await res.json()` against the same stream pulled the **full 25,165,846 bytes**, proving the cap is load-bearing.
  - Small well-formed bodies still decode: a `model not found` error drives the GLM-4.7 fallback (endpoint `coding-global`, model `glm-4.7`) — classification unchanged.

```
[case 1] REAL detectZaiEndpoint vs oversized streaming 400 (no Content-Length)
  ok: probe returns null (status-only fallback, body cannot promote) — detected=null
  ok: server saw client close the socket early (stream cancelled) — clientClosedEarly=true
  ok: bytes on wire stayed below the full ~24 MiB body (stream cancelled early) — bytesSent=18612246 (cap=16777216, ceiling=25165824)
[negative control] OLD `await res.json()` buffers the whole body
  ok: unbounded read pulled the FULL ~24 MiB body (proving the cap is load-bearing) — drained=25165846 >= ceiling=25165824
[case 2] REAL readResponseWithLimit(cap=16 MiB) rejects oversized body
  ok: readResponseWithLimit threw on overflow — message=Z.AI probe error body exceeded size limit (16777216 bytes)
  ok: stream cancelled early (below full body), not drained to ceiling — bytesSent=18481174 (ceiling=25165824)
[case 3] REAL detectZaiEndpoint classifies small well-formed error bodies
  ok: model-not-found errors decode + drive GLM-4.7 fallback (endpoint) — endpoint=coding-global
  ok: fallback model resolved from decoded body classification — modelId=glm-4.7

ALL PROOF ASSERTIONS PASSED
```

- **Mutation / load-bearing control**: temporarily reverting the fix to the old fail-open `await res.json()` makes the oversized test fail (`AssertionError: expected false to be true` — stream not cancelled, body drained to the 64 MiB ceiling); restoring the real fix returns all 6 green. The new test genuinely guards the bound.
- **What was not tested**: no live Z.AI endpoint was hit (no key would reproduce a hostile oversized body). The 64-bit OOM end-state was not driven to crash; the proof measures bytes-on-wire + early socket close + a negative control, which is the load-bearing signal.

## Evidence

Focused tests (local mocks only; the shared `provider-http-test-mocks.ts` is intentionally untouched to avoid cross-test blast radius):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose \
    --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts \
    extensions/zai/detect.test.ts

 ✓ detectZaiEndpoint > resolves preferred/fallback endpoints and null when probes fail
 ✓ detectZaiEndpoint > caps oversized probe timeouts before scheduling
 ✓ detectZaiEndpoint > still parses well-formed sub-cap error bodies to drive endpoint classification
 ✓ detectZaiEndpoint > swallows malformed and empty sub-cap error bodies and falls back on status
 ✓ detectZaiEndpoint > fails closed on oversized probe error bodies without buffering unbounded
 ✓ detectZaiEndpoint > rejects oversized bodies via the shared bounded reader the probe uses

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

The two oversized tests drive the **real** `detectZaiEndpoint` (and the real `readResponseWithLimit`) with a `ReadableStream` instrumented to record bytes pulled and stream cancellation; the happy-path and malformed/empty tests prove well-formed and small malformed bodies behave exactly as before. `oxfmt --check` clean and `oxlint` clean on both changed files.

**Label**: security

_AI-assisted:_ implemented with **Claude Code** (Anthropic). Scope of assistance: diagnosing the unbounded-read defect, locating the existing `readResponseWithLimit` convention, the code edit, authoring the focused tests + the oversized-stream mock, building the real `node:http` proof harness with negative + mutation controls, and running local validation (vitest + oxfmt + oxlint). All changes were reviewed by a human before submission.
